findings.description: Calling .items() on a potentially None return — NoneType has no attribute items
findings.fix_hint: Ensure the object is not None before calling .items(), or use (x or {}).items()
findings.test_hint: Verify .items() is not called on functions that may return None

findings.description: Debug print statement left in code — use logging instead
findings.fix_hint: Replace print() with logging.debug/info/warning as appropriate

findings.description: Silent exception swallowing — error caught but ignored
findings.fix_hint: At minimum log the error; better: handle or re-raise

findings.description: Direct os.environ[] access crashes on missing key — use .get() with default
findings.fix_hint: Use os.environ.get('KEY', 'default') instead of os.environ['KEY']
findings.test_hint: Verify environment variable access has defaults for missing keys

findings.fix_hint: Wrap all dynamic values with an HTML escape function or use textContent
findings.test_hint: Verify _escHtml or equivalent sanitizer wraps all dynamic values in innerHTML assignments

findings.description: sys.exit() in library code — kills the entire process, prevents reuse
findings.fix_hint: Raise an exception instead; let the caller decide how to handle failure
findings.test_hint: Verify library functions raise exceptions instead of calling sys.exit()

findings.description: Command injection: subprocess called with shell=True
findings.fix_hint: Use shell=False and pass args as a list
findings.test_hint: Verify subprocess calls do not use shell=True with user-controlled input

findings.description: Code injection: eval/exec with potentially untrusted input
findings.fix_hint: Replace eval/exec with safe alternatives (ast.literal_eval, JSON.parse)
findings.test_hint: Verify no eval/exec is used on user-controlled data

findings.description: Unchecked int() on user input — will crash on non-numeric values
findings.fix_hint: Wrap in try/except ValueError with a sensible default
findings.test_hint: Test that non-numeric input to this parameter returns a default, not a 500

findings.description: Unchecked int() on user input — will crash on non-numeric values

findings.description: Unchecked float() on user input — will crash on non-numeric values

findings.description: JSON parsing without error handling — crashes on malformed input
findings.fix_hint: Wrap json.loads() in try/except json.JSONDecodeError
findings.test_hint: Test that malformed JSON input returns an error response, not a crash

findings.description: JSON parsing without error handling — crashes on malformed input
findings.fix_hint: Wrap json.loads() in try/except json.JSONDecodeError
findings.test_hint: Test that malformed JSON input returns an error response, not a crash

findings.description: File opened without explicit encoding — platform-dependent behavior
findings.fix_hint: Always specify encoding='utf-8' for text file operations
findings.test_hint: Verify all text file opens specify explicit encoding

findings.description: Code injection: eval/exec with potentially untrusted input
findings.fix_hint: Replace eval/exec with safe alternatives (ast.literal_eval, JSON.parse)
findings.test_hint: Verify no eval/exec is used on user-controlled data

findings.description: Catching broad Exception — masks bugs and makes debugging hard
findings.fix_hint: Catch specific exceptions (ValueError, TypeError, OSError) instead of Exception
findings.test_hint: Verify except clauses catch the narrowest possible exception type

findings.description: Command injection: subprocess called with shell=True
findings.fix_hint: Use shell=False and pass args as a list
findings.test_hint: Verify subprocess calls do not use shell=True with user-controlled input

findings.description: Hardcoded user-specific path — breaks on any other machine
findings.fix_hint: Replace with Path.home() or os.environ.get('ENV_VAR', fallback). For test fixtures use Path(__file__).resolve().parent / 'subdir'
findings.test_hint: Verify no C:\Users\<username> paths exist in production code

findings.description: Hardcoded C:\AI\ path — not portable; use ZENAI_MODEL_DIR env var or Path.home() / 'AI' fallback
findings.fix_hint: Use os.environ.get('ZENAI_MODEL_DIR', str(Path.home() / 'AI' / 'Models'))
findings.test_hint: Verify model/binary paths use env vars with home-based fallbacks

findings.description: Hardcoded absolute Windows path — not portable to Linux/macOS
findings.fix_hint: Use Path(__file__).parent-relative paths or environment variables
findings.test_hint: Verify no hardcoded drive-letter paths outside standard system dirs

findings.description: Hardcoded absolute Windows path — not portable to Linux/macOS
findings.fix_hint: Use Path(__file__).parent-relative paths or environment variables
findings.test_hint: Verify no hardcoded drive-letter paths outside standard system dirs

findings.description: Windows-only module import — will crash on Linux/macOS unless guarded by platform check
findings.fix_hint: Guard with: if platform.system() == 'Windows': import winreg  or use try/except ImportError
findings.test_hint: Verify Windows-only imports are guarded by platform checks or try/except

findings.description: Windows-only module import — will crash on Linux/macOS unless guarded by platform check
findings.fix_hint: Guard with: if platform.system() == 'Windows': import winreg  or use try/except ImportError
findings.test_hint: Verify Windows-only imports are guarded by platform checks or try/except

findings.description: Windows-only module import — will crash on Linux/macOS unless guarded by platform check
findings.fix_hint: Guard with: if platform.system() == 'Windows': import winreg  or use try/except ImportError
findings.test_hint: Verify Windows-only imports are guarded by platform checks or try/except

findings.test_hint: Verify no bare except clauses exist — all should name specific exceptions

findings.description: Silent exception swallowing — error caught but ignored
findings.fix_hint: At minimum log the error; better: handle or re-raise

findings.description: Non-daemon thread may prevent clean shutdown
findings.fix_hint: Set daemon=True for background worker threads
findings.test_hint: Verify background threads are daemon threads so process can exit cleanly

findings.description: Long sleep (10+ seconds) in code — may indicate polling instead of events
findings.fix_hint: Use threading.Event, asyncio, or callback patterns instead of long sleeps
findings.test_hint: Verify sleeps are short or replaced with event-driven patterns

findings.description: Long sleep (10+ seconds) in code — may indicate polling instead of events
findings.fix_hint: Use threading.Event, asyncio, or callback patterns instead of long sleeps
findings.test_hint: Verify sleeps are short or replaced with event-driven patterns

findings.description: Explicit keep-alive header — may cause connection hang in some HTTP servers
findings.fix_hint: Remove Connection: keep-alive header; let the server/framework handle it
findings.test_hint: Test that HTTP responses close correctly without hanging

findings.description: Function annotated as -> None but returns a dict
findings.fix_hint: Fix the return type annotation to match actual return value
findings.test_hint: Verify function return types match their annotations

findings.description: Wildcard import pollutes namespace and hides dependencies
findings.fix_hint: Import specific names: from module import func1, func2
findings.test_hint: Verify no wildcard imports exist in production code

findings.test_hint: Test that malformed JSON input returns an error response, not a crash

findings.test_hint: Verify all text file opens specify explicit encoding

findings.description: Code injection: eval/exec with potentially untrusted input
findings.fix_hint: Replace eval/exec with safe alternatives (ast.literal_eval, JSON.parse)
findings.test_hint: Verify no eval/exec is used on user-controlled data

findings.description: Bare except clause swallows all errors including KeyboardInterrupt
findings.fix_hint: Catch specific exceptions: except (ValueError, TypeError):
findings.test_hint: Verify no bare except clauses exist — all should name specific exceptions

findings.description: Direct os.environ[] access crashes on missing key — use .get() with default
findings.fix_hint: Use os.environ.get('KEY', 'default') instead of os.environ['KEY']
findings.test_hint: Verify environment variable access has defaults for missing keys

findings.description: Silent exception swallowing — error caught but ignored
findings.fix_hint: At minimum log the error; better: handle or re-raise
findings.test_hint: Verify caught exceptions are logged or handled, not silently passed

findings.description: Debug print statement left in code — use logging instead
findings.fix_hint: Replace print() with logging.debug/info/warning as appropriate
findings.test_hint: Verify production code uses logging module instead of print()

findings.description: sys.exit() in library code — kills the entire process, prevents reuse
findings.fix_hint: Raise an exception instead; let the caller decide how to handle failure
findings.test_hint: Verify library functions raise exceptions instead of calling sys.exit()

findings.description: Windows-only module import — will crash on Linux/macOS unless guarded by platform check
findings.fix_hint: Guard with: if platform.system() == 'Windows': import winreg  or use try/except ImportError
findings.test_hint: Verify Windows-only imports are guarded by platform checks or try/except
